name: search
description:
Search for events in Google Calendar.

Use time_min/time_max for time-based questions ("what meetings do I have today", "schedule for next week") — compute actual RFC3339 timestamps with the user's timezone offset from viewer context (e.g. 2026-05-20T00:00:00-07:00). Always include the offset.

IMPORTANT — query is simple text matching, NOT a search engine:
- Use a SINGLE keyword or short phrase (e.g. "dentist", "team sync"). Do NOT use OR, AND, NOT operators — they are matched literally and will return empty results.
- Prefer time_min/time_max alone to list events in a range, then filter results yourself. Only add query when you need to narrow by a specific word in the event title/description.
- If a search returns empty, retry with a broader time range or drop the query entirely.

To search another user's calendar, pass their email as calendar_id.
Events returned contain an event_id, which is required for other Google Calendar tools.

query.description: Simple text match against event title, description, location, and attendees. Use a single keyword or short phrase (e.g. 'dentist', 'team sync'). Do NOT use OR/AND/NOT operators — they are matched literally. Omit to list all events in a time range.

max_results.description: Maximum number of events to return (default 25, max 100)

time_min.description: Start of time range (RFC3339 with timezone offset, e.g. 1970-01-31T12:00:00-08:00). Always include the user's timezone offset from viewer context.

time_max.description: End of time range (RFC3339 with timezone offset, e.g. 1970-01-31T13:00:00-08:00). Always include the user's timezone offset from viewer context.

calendar_id.description: Calendar ID to search (e.g. email address from google_calendar_list_calendars). Defaults to the user's primary calendar.
